selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","opinion":"Look, when you’re knee-deep in work, especially running your own shop like I do, time is money. And frankly, arguing endlessly on the internet or even just at the coffee shop counter isn't productive—it's just draining. Dealing with these talks about healthcare and stuff like COVID vaccines can feel like walking into a brawl when all you want to do is get back to fixing an engine. The key, from where I sit, is knowing when to pivot and when to hold your ground, but mostly, it’s about protecting your own peace. You've got a high openness score, which means you're curious and willing to look at different angles, but that also makes these conversations tricky because everyone has an angle now. 

I find that if the discussion starts turning into name-calling or just shouting accusations—the kind of thing where people aren’t talking about facts anymore, but feelings or political loyalties—you gotta treat it like a broken tool: you unplug it. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
Look, when you’re knee-deep in work, especially running your own shop like I do, time is money. And frankly, arguing endlessly on the internet or even just at the coffee shop counter isn't productive—it's just draining. Dealing with these talks about healthcare and stuff like COVID vaccines can feel like walking into a brawl when all you want to do is get back to fixing an engine. The key, from where I sit, is knowing when to pivot and when to hold your ground, but mostly, it’s about protecting your own peace. You've got a high openness score, which means you're curious and willing to look at different angles, but that also makes these conversations tricky because everyone has an angle now. 

I find that if the discussion starts turning into name-calling or just shouting accusations—the kind of thing where people aren’t talking about facts anymore, but feelings or political loyalties—you gotta treat it like a broken tool: you unplug it. You politely state something like, “We're getting off track,” or
